title: Friday Sermon 9 February 2001
description:
O ye who believe!  Avoid too frequent indulgence in suspicion; for some suspicions are certainly sin.  Spy not nor backbite one another.  Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his dead brother?  Indeed you would abhor the very thought.  So be mindful of Allah, surely Allah is Oft-Relenting and is Merciful.

('The Holy Quran'.  Al Hujrat [The Inner Chambers]: 13).

The Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be on him) said that to have a good opinion and a favourable view was a beautiful worship.

(Abu Dawud Sulayman ibn Ashath.  'Sunan Abu Dawud').

Three days prior to passing away, the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be on him) said that each believer should depart this life having a favourable view of Allah the Almighty.

(Ahmad ibn Hanbal.  'Musnad Ahmad ibn Hanbal').

As the time of death is in no one's control, it is essential to live each moment in a state of faith and having a favourable view of Allah the Almighty:

Many ills are borne out of suspicion.  Unless one has an absolute knowledge about something it should not be believed.

(Hazrat Mirza Ghulam Ahmad – The Promised Messiah & Mahdi.  29th December 1905.  'Malfoozat: iv'.  654).

‘Suspicion destroys man's belief and hurls one away from uprightness.  To achieve the excellence of the Truthful it is essential to really avoid suspicion and to profusely seek Allah's forgiveness if any doubts are formed about someone.  Suspicion negates the virtues of Prayer, fasting and almsgiving.  Allah the Almighty spoils the impression about Himself for those who have suspicion about his servants.  The entire Quran instructs us not to be suspicious of God and not to separate oneself from God and to only seek help from Him.’ 

The majority of reports I receive, regarding hostilities, are based upon suspicion.
